title: OpenKYCAML Hybrid KYC/AML Schema
description: Root OpenKYCAML document. At least one of 'ivms101' or 'verifiableCredential' MUST be present.

NaturalPersonNameIdentifier.title: NaturalPersonNameIdentifier
NaturalPersonNameIdentifier.description: Structured name components for a natural person (IVMS 101 §3.2).

primaryIdentifier.description: Family name / surname of the natural person. Maps to eIDAS 2.0 PID attribute 'family_name' when nameIdentifierType is LEGL.

secondaryIdentifier.description: Given name(s) / forename(s) of the natural person. Maps to eIDAS 2.0 PID attribute 'given_name'.

addressLine.description: Unstructured address lines (up to 7), used when structured fields are unavailable.

nationalIdentifier.description: Identifier value (e.g., passport number, tax ID). Maps to eIDAS 2.0 PID attribute 'person_identifier' or 'personal_administrative_number'. For legal entities with LEIX type, maps to eIDAS 2.0 LPID attribute 'unique_identifier'.

countryOfIssue.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of issuing country.

registrationAuthority.description: Registration authority (required when nationalIdentifierType is LEIX).

verifyingDocumentRef.description: Reference to the document record in identityDocuments.naturalPersonDocuments[] or identityDocuments.legalEntityDocuments[] (by documentRef URI) that provided evidence for this identifier. Enables traceability from identifier to source document.

registrationAuthorityDetail.description: Structured GLEIF RAL reference to the registration authority, replacing or supplementing the free-text registrationAuthority field. Required when nationalIdentifierType is LEIX or ISO_20275_ELF_CODE.

DateAndPlaceOfBirth.title: DateAndPlaceOfBirth
DateAndPlaceOfBirth.description: Birth information for a natural person (IVMS 101 §3.3).

dateOfBirth.description: Date of birth in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Maps to eIDAS 2.0 PID attribute 'birth_date'.

placeOfBirth.description: City or municipality of birth. Maps to eIDAS 2.0 PID attribute 'birth_place' (optional in eIDAS; required by IVMS 101 §3.3).

nameIdentifier.description: One or more name identifiers for the natural person.

geographicAddress.description: Geographic address(es) of the natural person. Maps to eIDAS 2.0 PID attribute 'resident_address' (structured).

customerIdentification.description: Internal customer reference number assigned by the originating VASP/institution.

countryOfResidence.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country of residence. Maps to eIDAS 2.0 PID attribute 'resident_country'.

nationality.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 nationality of the natural person. Maps to eIDAS 2.0 PID attribute 'nationality'. Use when nationality differs from country of residence.

LegalPersonNameIdentifier.title: LegalPersonNameIdentifier
LegalPersonNameIdentifier.description: Structured name identifier for a legal person (IVMS 101 §3.8).

legalPersonName.description: Registered legal name of the entity. Maps to eIDAS 2.0 LPID attribute 'current_legal_name' when legalPersonNameIdentifierType is LEGL.

LegalPersonIdentificationData.title: LegalPersonIdentificationData
LegalPersonIdentificationData.description: eIDAS 2.0 Legal Person Identification Data (LPID) as defined in the EU Digital Identity Architecture and Reference Framework (ARF). Issued by Member State-appointed PID Providers for legal entities and accepted under AMLR Articles 22 and 26 as high-assurance electronic identification.

currentLegalName.description: Current legal name of the entity as registered. Mandatory LPID attribute. Maps to legalPerson.name.nameIdentifier[0].legalPersonName (LEGL).

uniqueIdentifier.description: Member State-issued unique identifier for the legal entity, constructed for cross-border compatibility per eIDAS 2.0 ARF. Mandatory LPID attribute. Maps to legalPerson.nationalIdentification.nationalIdentifier.

currentAddress.description: Current registered address of the legal entity. Optional LPID attribute. Maps to legalPerson.geographicAddress[].

vatRegistrationNumber.description: VAT registration number of the legal entity. Optional LPID attribute.

taxReferenceNumber.description: Tax reference number of the legal entity. Optional LPID attribute.

europeanUniqueIdentifier.description: European Unique Identifier (EUID) per Directive (EU) 2017/1132 (company register cross-border identifier). Optional LPID attribute.

lei.description: Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) per ISO 17442. Optional LPID attribute. Maps to legalPerson.lei and/or legalPerson.nationalIdentification with nationalIdentifierType LEIX.

eoriNumber.description: Economic Operators Registration and Identification (EORI) number. Optional LPID attribute used for customs and trade identification.

mandates.description: Representation and mandate records — natural persons authorised to act on behalf of the legal entity. Supports AMLR authorised representative and beneficial owner verification. May be conveyed as QEAAs in EUDI Wallet flows.

naturalPerson.description: The natural person holding the mandate or representation power.

roleOrPower.description: Description of the role or power of representation (e.g. 'Director', 'Authorised Signatory', 'Power of Attorney').

validFrom.description: Date from which the mandate is effective (ISO 8601).

validUntil.description: Date on which the mandate expires (ISO 8601). Omit if open-ended.

geographicAddress.description: Geographic address(es) of the legal person. Maps to eIDAS 2.0 LPID attribute 'current_address'.

customerNumber.description: Internal customer or account reference number assigned by the originating VASP/institution to the legal entity. Also used to carry optional eIDAS 2.0 LPID attributes such as VAT number, tax reference, or EORI number when applicable. To distinguish identifier types use a recognised prefix followed by a colon, e.g. 'VAT:GB123456789', 'EORI:GB123456789000', or 'TAX:DE123456789'.

lei.description: Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) per ISO 17442. Maps directly to eIDAS 2.0 LPID attribute 'lei'. Also expressible via nationalIdentification with nationalIdentifierType LEIX; this field provides an explicit top-level mapping for LPID interoperability.

countryOfRegistration.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country of registration. Derivable from the LEI or unique_identifier in eIDAS 2.0 LPID.

lpid.description: Optional eIDAS 2.0 Legal Person Identification Data (LPID) block. Carries LPID-specific attributes (current legal name, unique identifier, VAT, EORI, LEI, mandates) for EUDI Wallet and AMLR high-assurance CDD flows. Backward-compatible — omit for pure IVMS 101 usage.

legalFormCode.description: ISO 20275:2017 Entity Legal Form (ELF) code as published by GLEIF (https://www.gleif.org/en/about-lei/code-lists/iso-20275-entity-legal-forms-code-list). Four-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the entity's legal form within its jurisdiction (e.g. '8888' = US LLC, 'Z13E' = UK PLC, '2HBR' = Jersey Discretionary Trust). Mandatory for GLEIF LEI registrations. Recommended for FATF Rec. 24/25 and AMLR Art. 26 compliance.

legalFormDescription.description: Free-text description of the entity's legal form. Use when the ISO 20275 ELF code is unavailable, the jurisdiction is not yet in the GLEIF ELF code list, or additional context beyond the code is needed (e.g. 'Discretionary Trust governed by Jersey law'). Should complement, not replace, legalFormCode where the code is known.

entityType.description: High-level classification of the legal arrangement type. Drives the corresponding typed sub-object (trustDetails, foundationDetails, partnershipDetails). Required for FATF Rec. 25 and AMLR Art. 26(2)(b) typed disclosure of legal arrangements. Use COMPANY for ordinary corporate entities where the ELF code alone is sufficient.

trustDetails.description: Typed trust-arrangement sub-object. Present when entityType is TRUST. Captures the four FATF Rec. 25 / AMLR Art. 26(2)(b) principal categories: settlor, trustee, protector, and beneficiary class.

foundationDetails.description: Typed foundation sub-object. Present when entityType is FOUNDATION. Captures founders, council members, and foundation purpose per FATF Rec. 25 / AMLR Art. 26.

partnershipDetails.description: Typed partnership sub-object. Present when entityType is PARTNERSHIP. Distinguishes LP, LLP, and GP structures and records general and limited partners per FATF Rec. 24/25.

otherEntityDescription.description: Free-text description of the legal arrangement when entityType is OTHER and no typed sub-object applies. Should describe the nature, form, and governing law of the arrangement.

verifyingDocumentRef.description: Reference to the LegalEntityDocument record (by documentRef URI) in identityDocuments.legalEntityDocuments[] that confirmed the legalFormCode via the GLEIF RAL. Typically a REGISTRY_EXTRACT or CERTIFICATE_OF_INCORPORATION record.

TrustDetails.title: TrustDetails
TrustDetails.description: Typed sub-object for trust arrangements. Captures the four principal categories required by FATF Recommendation 25 and AMLR Art. 26(2)(b): settlors, trustees, protectors, and beneficiary class. All person arrays accept natural persons and/or legal persons (e.g. a corporate trustee).

settlors.description: Persons (natural or legal) who settled the trust assets. FATF Rec. 25 requires identification of the settlor.

trustees.description: Persons (natural or legal) holding legal title and administering the trust. FATF Rec. 25 requires identification of each trustee.

protectors.description: Persons holding a power to direct or remove trustees (protector role). Required for AMLR Art. 26(2)(b) where a protector exists.

beneficiaryClass.description: Description of the class of beneficiaries (e.g. 'Named beneficiaries: Alice Smith, Bob Smith; remainder class: lawful descendants'). Use when individual beneficiaries cannot be fully enumerated at onboarding. FATF Rec. 25 permits description of the class where beneficiaries are not yet determined.

namedBeneficiaries.description: Individually named beneficiaries where determinable. Should supplement beneficiaryClass where both apply.

dateEstablished.description: Date the trust was settled or constituted.

jurisdictionOfLaw.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the governing law jurisdiction (e.g. 'JE' for Jersey, 'KY' for Cayman Islands).

trustDeedReference.description: Reference number or identifier for the trust deed or settlement document.

FoundationDetails.title: FoundationDetails
FoundationDetails.description: Typed sub-object for foundation legal arrangements. Captures founders, council members, and foundation purpose per FATF Recommendation 25 and AMLR Art. 26.

founders.description: Persons (natural or legal) who established the foundation.

councilMembers.description: Members of the foundation council or supervisory board.

foundationPurpose.description: Objects or purpose of the foundation as stated in its constitutional documents (e.g. charitable, family wealth preservation, private interest).

dateEstablished.description: Date the foundation was established or registered.

jurisdictionOfRegistration.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code where the foundation is registered.

PartnershipDetails.title: PartnershipDetails
PartnershipDetails.description: Typed sub-object for partnership arrangements (LP, LLP, GP). Records general and limited partners per FATF Recommendation 24/25.

partnershipType.description: Structural sub-type of the partnership. LP = Limited Partnership; LLP = Limited Liability Partnership; GP = General Partnership.

generalPartners.description: General partners who bear unlimited liability and manage the partnership. Required for LP and GP structures under FATF Rec. 24.

limitedPartners.description: Limited partners whose liability is restricted to their capital contribution. Present for LP structures.

members.description: Members of an LLP (used instead of generalPartners/limitedPartners for LLP structures where all members share limited liability).

dateEstablished.description: Date the partnership was formed or registered.

jurisdictionOfRegistration.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code where the partnership is registered.

Originator.title: Originator
Originator.description: IVMS 101 originator — the sending party in a virtual asset transfer.

accountNumber.description: Account numbers or virtual asset wallet addresses of the originator.

Beneficiary.title: Beneficiary
Beneficiary.description: IVMS 101 beneficiary — the receiving party in a virtual asset transfer.

accountNumber.description: Account numbers or virtual asset wallet addresses of the beneficiary.

name.description: Registered legal or trading name of the VASP.

vaspIdentifier.description: Internal or network-assigned identifier for this VASP.

lei.description: Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) of the VASP (ISO 17442).

IVMS101Payload.title: IVMS101Payload
IVMS101Payload.description: Root IVMS 101 Travel Rule message payload.

originatingVASP.description: The VASP transmitting the Travel Rule message.

beneficiaryVASP.description: The VASP receiving the Travel Rule message.

transferPath.description: Ordered list of intermediate VASPs in the transfer chain (IVMS 101 §3.15).

transactionIdentifier.description: Unique identifier for this transaction assigned by the originating VASP.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime when this payload was created.

transferredAmount.description: Value and asset type of the transfer.

amount.description: Transfer amount as a decimal string.

assetType.description: Asset type code (e.g., BTC, ETH, USD, EUR).

VerifiableCredentialProof.title: VerifiableCredentialProof
VerifiableCredentialProof.description: Cryptographic proof section conforming to W3C VC Data Model v1.1 / v2-compatible structure / eIDAS 2.0 ARF.

created.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the proof was created.

verificationMethod.description: DID URL of the key used to create the proof.

jws.description: JSON Web Signature (compact serialization) for JsonWebSignature2020.

VerifiableCredentialWrapper.title: VerifiableCredentialWrapper
VerifiableCredentialWrapper.description: Optional W3C Verifiable Credential envelope conforming to W3C VC Data Model v1.1 (v2-compatible structure) and eIDAS 2.0 ARF §6.3. When present, the IVMS101 payload and kycProfile are transported as credentialSubject. Note: the @context[0] uses the W3C VC DM v1.1 URL; migration to the v2 URL is planned for v2.0.0.

id.description: Unique identifier for this Verifiable Credential.

issuer.description: DID or URL of the issuing institution.

expirationDate.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the credential expires.

credentialSubject.description: The subject of this credential. Embeds the IVMS 101 Travel Rule payload and/or the KYC profile directly so that a single signed VC carries all required AML identity data for eIDAS 2.0 / EUDIW wallet flows. Maps to W3C VC Data Model v1.1 credentialSubject.

id.description: DID of the credential subject (the customer or legal entity).

ivms101.description: Full IVMS 101 Travel Rule payload embedded inside the VC for hybrid Travel-Rule / eIDAS 2.0 use. Enables mapping to eIDAS PID / LPID attributes without losing FATF Rec 16 compliance.

kycProfile.description: Optional KYC/AML profile embedded inside the VC, enabling CDD reliance and risk-data portability across institutions via a signed attestation.

lpid.description: Optional eIDAS 2.0 LPID block at the VC credentialSubject level. Enables a standalone LPID Verifiable Credential for legal entity identification in EUDI Wallet flows without requiring a full IVMS 101 payload.

type.description: Revocation status type. 'StatusList2021Entry' for W3C Status List 2021; 'RevocationList2020Status' for earlier W3C spec; 'AnonCredsCredentialStatusList2023' for Hyperledger AnonCreds CL_ACCUM accumulator-based revocation (Sovrin/Indy).

credentialSchema.description: Schema identifier for this credential. For W3C JSON Schema credentials use type 'JsonSchemaValidator2018'. For Hyperledger AnonCreds (Sovrin/Indy) credentials use type 'AnonCredsDefinition' with the Sovrin ledger schema ID in 'id' and the Credential Definition ID in 'credDefId'.

id.description: URI of the credential schema. For JSON Schema credentials: an HTTPS URI resolving to the JSON Schema. For AnonCreds credentials: the Sovrin/Indy schema ledger identifier in the format '<issuerDID>:2:<schemaName>:<schemaVersion>' (e.g. 'Gs6cQcvrtWoZKsbBhD3dQJ:2:KYCCredential:1.0').

type.description: Schema validator type. 'JsonSchemaValidator2018' for W3C JSON Schema credentials; 'AnonCredsDefinition' for Hyperledger AnonCreds / Sovrin credentials.

credDefId.description: Hyperledger AnonCreds / Sovrin Credential Definition ID. Format: '<issuerDID>:3:<signatureType>:<schemaSeqNo>:<tag>' (e.g. 'Gs6cQcvrtWoZKsbBhD3dQJ:3:CL:1234:default'). Only required when type is 'AnonCredsDefinition'. Uniquely identifies the public key and non-revocation parameters used to issue the AnonCreds credential.

evidence.description: W3C VC §6.4 evidence block. Used in EUDI Wallet flows to reference the source credential(s) — typically a PID or LPID — that were presented by the subject and from which the IVMS 101 / kycProfile data was derived. Enables triangulation of the identity chain: PID Issuer → Subject Wallet → VASP.

id.description: Unique identifier of the source credential or presentation event.

verifier.description: DID of the relying party / verifier that received the original presentation (typically the VASP).

credentialIssuer.description: DID of the authority that originally issued the source credential (e.g. national PID Provider, Bundesanzeiger LPID Issuer).

evidenceDocument.description: Human-readable description of the source document or credential (e.g. 'German National Identity Card (eIDAS 2.0 PID)').

subjectPresence.description: Whether the subject was physically or digitally present during the presentation.

documentPresence.description: Whether the document was presented physically or digitally.

presentationDate.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the credential was presented.

selectiveDisclosure.description: SD-JWT VC selective disclosure metadata (IETF draft-ietf-oauth-sd-jwt-vc / ARF §6.3.4). Present when this credential is issued or presented as an SD-JWT VC.

_sd_alg.description: Hash algorithm used for SD-JWT disclosure digests (sha-256 required by ARF).

disclosableClaimPaths.description: JSON Pointer paths (RFC 6901) of claims that MAY be selectively withheld. FATF-mandatory fields MUST NOT appear here.

requiredClaimPaths.description: JSON Pointer paths always revealed — never withheld. Includes FATF Travel Rule mandatory fields.

decodedDisclosures.description: Human-readable decoded SD-JWT disclosures for documentation/reference. In an actual SD-JWT the values are base64url-encoded and the claim values are replaced by their SHA-256 digests.

claimName.description: Name of the claim being disclosed.

sdJwtNote.description: Human-readable note about the SD-JWT presentation context.

isPEP.description: Whether the subject is classified as a PEP.

isPep.description: Alias for isPEP (reference schema interoperability). When both are present, isPEP takes precedence.

pepCategory.description: PEP classification category aligned to the AMLA draft RTS taxonomy (AMLR Art. 28–31). Primary values: DOMESTIC_PEP, FOREIGN_PEP, INTERNATIONAL_ORGANISATION, FAMILY_MEMBER_OF_PEP, CLOSE_ASSOCIATE_OF_PEP, FORMER_PEP, NOT_PEP. Legacy aliases INTERNATIONAL_ORG_PEP and RCA are retained for backward compatibility.

pepRole.description: Specific role or position that triggers PEP classification.

screeningDate.description: Date of the most recent PEP screening.

screeningProvider.description: Name of the screening data provider.

status.description: Alias for screeningStatus (reference schema interoperability). Accepts all screeningStatus values.

screeningProvider.description: Sanctions list provider (e.g., Refinitiv, Dow Jones, Comply Advantage).

listsChecked.description: Sanctions lists checked (e.g., OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated, UN Consolidated, HMT).

hits.description: Names or identifiers of matched sanctions entries.

AdverseMedia.title: AdverseMedia
AdverseMedia.description: Adverse media / negative news screening result.

hasHits.description: Whether any adverse media hits were found.

lastCheckedDate.description: Date of the most recent adverse media check (reference schema interoperability).

summary.description: Brief summary of adverse media findings.

SourceOfFundsWealth.title: SourceOfFundsWealth
SourceOfFundsWealth.description: Declared and verified source of funds and source of wealth.

sourceOfFunds.description: Declared source(s) of funds for the account or transaction.

sourceOfWealthNarrative.description: Free-text description of how wealth was accumulated.

verificationStatus.description: Verification status of the declared source of funds/wealth.

then.description: When 'person' is present, the legacy 'naturalPerson' field must not be used to avoid ambiguity.

person.description: The beneficial owner as a natural or legal person (reference schema interoperability).

naturalPerson.description: The beneficial owner as a natural person. Legacy field — use 'person' when natural or legal person support is needed. Do not use alongside 'person'.

ownershipType.description: Nature of the ownership or control.

controlMechanism.description: Mechanism by which control is exercised.

ownershipChainDepth.description: Number of tiers in the ownership chain: 1 = UBO owns subject entity directly, 2+ = ownership via one or more intermediate holding entities.

intermediateEntities.description: Ordered chain of intermediate holding entities between this UBO and the subject legal entity (AMLR Art. 26 enhanced transparency). First element is the entity directly holding an interest in the subject; last element is the entity directly owned by the UBO.

ownershipPercentage.description: Ownership or voting interest percentage held at this tier.

ownershipType.description: Nature of control at this tier.

controlMechanism.description: Mechanism by which control is exercised at this tier.

jurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country of incorporation of this entity.

verifyingDocumentRefs.description: References to document records (by documentRef URIs) in identityDocuments.legalEntityDocuments[] that evidence this beneficial ownership claim. Typically ULTIMATE_BENEFICIAL_OWNER_REGISTER, TRUST_DEED, or REGISTRY_EXTRACT records.

roleInArrangement.description: Role of this party within a legal arrangement (trust, foundation, or partnership) as required by FATF R.25 (2023 revision) and AMLR Art. 26. Extends the typed role fields in TrustDetails/FoundationDetails/PartnershipDetails sub-objects.

nomineeFlags.description: Nominee and transparency flags per FATF R.24/R.25 (2023 revisions) requiring identification of nominees acting on behalf of ultimate beneficial owners.

isNominee.description: True when this party is a nominee (acting on behalf of the actual UBO). Triggers enhanced verification requirements under FATF R.24 and AMLR Art. 26.

nominatorIdentified.description: True when the actual person(s) on whose behalf the nominee is acting have been identified and verified.

bearerShareFlag.description: True when the entity has issued bearer shares, creating opacity risk. FATF R.24 requires immobilisation or conversion of bearer shares.

trustInstrumentReference.description: Reference to the trust deed, foundation charter, or equivalent legal instrument (e.g. document URI, notary reference, or documentId URN from identityDocuments.legalEntityDocuments[]). Supports FATF R.25 requirement to identify and verify the legal arrangement instrument.

monitoringLevel.description: Intensity of the ongoing monitoring regime.

monitoringStatus.description: Current operational status of the business relationship. ACTIVE = relationship ongoing; SUSPENDED = temporarily restricted pending review; TERMINATED = relationship closed.

eddRequired.description: Whether Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) has been triggered for this customer. True when the customer is a PEP, is from a high-risk third country, or other EDD triggers under AMLR Article 22 apply.

lastReviewDate.description: Date of the last periodic review.

nextReviewDate.description: Date of the next scheduled review.

alertFrequency.description: Frequency at which alerts are generated for this customer. REAL_TIME = streaming transaction monitoring; ON_TRIGGER = alerts generated only when a rule fires.

ruleTriggerHistory.description: History of AML/CTF rule triggers fired for this customer, supporting AMLR Art. 21 ongoing monitoring audit requirements.

ruleId.description: Identifier of the transaction monitoring rule that triggered.

ruleDescription.description: Human-readable description of the triggered rule.

triggeredAt.description: ISO 8601 date-time when the rule triggered.

outcome.description: Disposition outcome for this rule trigger.

tmModelVersion.description: Version of the transaction monitoring (TM) rule engine or ML model in use when monitoring was last applied. Supports AMLR Art. 21 audit requirements and model lineage tracking for EU AI Act high-risk AML systems.

AuditMetadata.title: AuditMetadata
AuditMetadata.description: Audit trail and provenance metadata for this KYC record.

recordId.description: Globally unique identifier for this KYC/AML record.

recordCreatedAt.description: ISO 8601 datetime when this record was first created.

recordUpdatedAt.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the most recent update.

recordVersion.description: Monotonically increasing version number of this record.

createdBy.description: Identifier of the system or operator that created this record.

updatedBy.description: Identifier of the system or operator that last updated this record.

dataSourceSystem.description: Source system identifier (e.g., core banking system, CRM, KYC platform).

dataProvider.description: Name or DID of the institution that originally collected and verified the KYC data. Populated when this record is shared under AMLR Article 48 third-party reliance or a KYC utility arrangement.

sharingConsentReference.description: Reference to the consent or data-sharing agreement under which this record is being disclosed to a third party (e.g., reliance agreement ID, data-sharing framework reference). Required for AMLR Article 48 reliance and GDPR Article 6 lawful basis documentation.

dataRetentionDate.description: Date after which this record may be deleted per data retention policy.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime of this audit record (reference schema interoperability; see also recordCreatedAt).

TransactionMonitoring.title: TransactionMonitoring
TransactionMonitoring.description: Transaction-level AML monitoring data. Captures the outcome of automated rule evaluation, rule-triggered alerts, travel-rule compliance status, and any resulting regulatory reporting obligations (CTR, SAR/STR). Aligns with FATF Rec 16, EU AMLR Article 59, and national CTR/STR thresholds.

transactionReference.description: Internal or network-level transaction reference number.

monitoringDate.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the transaction was evaluated by the monitoring engine.

monitoringEngine.description: Name and version of the transaction monitoring system that produced this result.

overallOutcome.description: Aggregate outcome of all rules evaluated against this transaction.

rulesEvaluated.description: Results for each individual AML monitoring rule evaluated.

ruleId.description: Unique identifier of the monitoring rule.

ruleName.description: Human-readable name of the rule (e.g., 'Velocity – 7-day rolling window').

threshold.description: Configured threshold or parameter value for this rule (e.g., '10000 USD', '3 transactions / 7 days').

actualValue.description: Actual value observed for this transaction against the threshold.

notes.description: Analyst notes or system commentary on this rule result.

alerts.description: Transaction-level alerts generated by the monitoring engine.

alertDate.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the alert was generated.

riskScore.description: Numeric risk score assigned to this specific alert.

description.description: Narrative description of the alert and supporting rationale.

assignedTo.description: Analyst or team responsible for reviewing this alert.

reviewDate.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the alert review was completed.

travelRuleCompliance.description: Travel Rule (FATF Rec 16 / IVMS 101) compliance status for this transaction.

travelRuleApplicable.description: Whether the Travel Rule threshold is met and IVMS 101 data must be transmitted.

amount.description: Threshold value as a decimal string.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code or virtual asset code.

ivms101MessageId.description: UUID reference to the IVMS 101 message transmitted for this transaction.

beneficiaryVaspVerified.description: Whether the beneficiary VASP identity has been verified (e.g., via GLEIF, OpenVASP, or TRP).

reportingObligations.description: Regulatory reporting obligations triggered by this transaction (CTR, SAR/STR, etc.).

reportingJurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 jurisdiction where the report is filed.

reportingThreshold.description: Threshold that triggered the reporting obligation.

reportFiled.description: Whether the report has been filed with the relevant authority.

reportReference.description: Reference number assigned by the reporting institution or authority.

filingDate.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the report was filed.

filingAuthority.description: Name of the Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) or authority receiving the report.

ThirdPartyCDDReliance.title: ThirdPartyCDDReliance
ThirdPartyCDDReliance.description: Records the conditions under which CDD measures were performed by a third party and relied upon by the obliged entity. Aligns with AMLR Article 48(3): the obliged entity remains liable, must obtain immediate access to underlying data, and must document that the providing party meets AMLR Article 48(1) eligibility conditions.

relyingPartyId.description: Identifier (LEI, DID, or institution name) of the obliged entity relying on the third-party CDD.

providingPartyId.description: Identifier (LEI, DID, or institution name) of the third party that performed the CDD measures.

relianceScope.description: Due diligence tier that was performed by the providing party and is being relied upon.

relianceContractRef.description: Reference to the written arrangement or outsourcing contract between the relying and providing party as required by AMLR Article 48(3)(a).

relianceDate.description: Date on which reliance on the third-party CDD was formally established.

independentVerificationRequired.description: Whether the relying entity must perform any independent verification steps beyond accepting the providing party's results (e.g. additional EDD measures for high-risk customers).

accessToUnderlyingDataConfirmed.description: Confirms that the relying entity has obtained, or has documented right of immediate access to, the underlying CDD information and documentation held by the providing party, as required by AMLR Article 48(3)(b).

providingPartyEligibilityConfirmed.description: Confirms that the providing party has been assessed as meeting the eligibility conditions of AMLR Article 48(1) (i.e. is itself subject to equivalent AML/CFT obligations and supervision in its jurisdiction).

providingPartyJurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the jurisdiction in which the providing party is authorised and supervised.

liabilityNote.description: Free-text note documenting that ultimate CDD liability remains with the relying party, per AMLR Article 48(4).

DueDiligenceRequirements.title: DueDiligenceRequirements
DueDiligenceRequirements.description: Machine-readable record of which customer attributes were collected and verified at each due diligence tier. Enables downstream systems and supervisors to confirm that the obliged entity satisfied the attribute-level requirements expected under AMLR Article 22 and the forthcoming AMLA RTS on CDD attribute sets.

appliedTier.description: The due diligence tier that was applied to this customer.

verifiedAttributes.description: List of customer attributes that were collected and verified. Each entry names an attribute path within the ivms101 or kycProfile blocks (e.g. 'naturalPerson.name', 'naturalPerson.dateOfBirth', 'sourceOfFundsWealth').

verificationMethods.description: Methods used to verify the collected attributes.

assuranceLevel.description: eIDAS assurance level achieved, where applicable.

verificationProvider.description: Name or DID of the identity verification service or PID Provider that performed verification.

documentRef.description: Reference to the specific document in identityDocuments (by documentRef URI) that was the primary evidence source for this attribute verification step.

rtsComplianceNote.description: Free-text note documenting the basis for the attribute set and tier selection, pending publication of the AMLA RTS on CDD attribute sets under AMLR Article 22. Update this field once the binding RTS is in force.

KYCProfile.title: KYCProfile
KYCProfile.description: Full KYC/AML profile extending IVMS 101 with risk classification, screening, and monitoring data. Aligns with FATF Rec 10/16, EU AMLR Articles 22 and 26, and EBA Risk Factor Guidelines.

customerRiskRating.description: Overall customer risk rating. Required for FATF Rec 10 CDD and AMLR Article 22 risk-based approach.

riskRatingDetail.description: Detailed risk scoring breakdown. Use customerRiskRating for the top-level rating.

riskRatingDate.description: Date when the current risk rating was assigned.

riskFactors.description: Breakdown of individual risk factor scores.

ratingMethodology.description: Reference to the risk rating methodology applied.

customerClassification.description: Customer classification containing type, industry, and relationship purpose.

customerType.description: Regulatory or internal customer type classification.

industrySector.description: Industry or sector of the customer's primary business activity (e.g., REAL_ESTATE, CRYPTO_ASSETS, FINANCIAL_SERVICES).

occupationOrPurpose.description: Occupation (natural persons) or business purpose (legal entities) as declared by the customer.

relationshipPurpose.description: Nature and intended purpose of the business relationship (e.g., INVESTMENT, TRADE_FINANCE, PAYMENTS).

accreditedInvestor.description: Whether the investor has been assessed as an accredited, professional, or otherwise eligible investor under the applicable securities regulation. Maps to ERC-3643 claim topic 101 (Accredited / Eligible Investor). True = eligible to hold regulated/security tokens; False = retail investor, subject to stricter distribution rules.

investorCategoryJurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 jurisdiction code under which the investor category or accredited-investor determination was made (e.g. 'US' for SEC Rule 501, 'GB' for FCA Professional Client, 'EU' for MiFID II Professional Client).

dueDiligenceType.description: Type of due diligence applied per AMLR Article 22.

sourceOfFundsWealth.description: Declared source(s) of funds or wealth as free-text or coded values (e.g., EMPLOYMENT_INCOME, INHERITANCE). When sourceOfFundsWealthDetail is also present, it supersedes this field and the values here should match sourceOfFundsWealthDetail.sourceOfFunds.

sourceOfFundsWealthDetail.description: Detailed and verified source of funds and wealth record including supporting documents. Supersedes sourceOfFundsWealth when present.

beneficialOwnership.description: Ultimate Beneficial Owners (UBOs). Required for legal entities per AMLR Article 26.

onboardingChannel.description: Channel through which the customer was onboarded (relevant to AMLR Article 22 remote onboarding requirements).

kycCompletionDate.description: Date on which the initial KYC process was completed.

dueDiligenceRequirements.description: Structured record of which attributes were collected and verified at each DD tier. Supports AMLR Article 22 attribute-set obligations and the forthcoming AMLA RTS on CDD attribute sets.

thirdPartyCDDReliance.description: CDD reliance record when due diligence was performed by a third party. Required when onboardingChannel is THIRD_PARTY_CDD. Aligns with AMLR Article 48(3).

isEligible.description: Whether the investor was found eligible / whitelisted at the time of `eligibilityLastConfirmed`. This is the off-chain equivalent of the ERC-3643 `isVerified(address)` call in the Identity Registry: true = verified, KYC/AML checks passed, investor may hold the security token; false = not eligible or de-listed.

eligibilityLastConfirmed.description: Date on which the `isEligible` determination was last confirmed. Should be refreshed on each periodic review or re-KYC. Maps to the timestamp of the last `isVerified()` check in an ERC-3643 Identity Registry.

blockchainAccountIds.description: Blockchain wallet addresses associated with this investor. In ERC-3643, the `investorAddress` (Ethereum address) is the primary identifier in the Identity Registry; each address maps 1:1 to an ONCHAINID contract. This array enables multi-wallet and multi-chain registrations to be documented in a single KYC record.

address.description: The blockchain wallet address (e.g. Ethereum checksummed hex address '0x...' or Solana base58 address).

network.description: Network or chain identifier (e.g. 'ethereum', 'polygon', 'avalanche'). Use CAIP-2 chain IDs where available (e.g. 'eip155:1' for Ethereum mainnet, 'eip155:137' for Polygon).

onchainIDAddress.description: Address of the ONCHAINID smart contract (ERC-734/735) linked to this wallet in an ERC-3643 Identity Registry. This is the `identity` parameter in `IIdentityRegistry.registerIdentity(investorAddress, identity, country)`.

registeredAt.description: Date on which this wallet was registered in the Identity Registry.

isFrozen.description: Whether this wallet address is currently frozen in the associated ERC-3643 Identity Registry or token contract. Mirrors the `isFrozen(address)` state of the IERC3643 token.

frozenTokenAmount.description: Amount of tokens frozen on this wallet, as a decimal string. Mirrors `getFrozenTokens(address)` in IERC3643.

xrplCredentialType.description: XRPL on-ledger credential type associated with this wallet, stored as a hexadecimal string (1–64 bytes, 2–128 hex characters, case-insensitive). Mirrors the `CredentialType` field of an XRPL `Credential` ledger object (XLS-70). Enables round-trip reconstruction of the on-ledger XRPL credential without relying on external state. The decoded human-readable equivalent is captured in `verifiableCredential.type[]`. Examples: '4b594343726564656e7469616c' (KYCCredential), '4f70656e4b5943414d4c43726564656e7469616c' (OpenKYCAMLCredential).

mptIssuanceId.description: XRPL Multi-Purpose Token issuance identifier (MPTokenIssuanceID) for which this wallet holds or has been authorised, stored as a 48-character hexadecimal string (case-insensitive; Hash192 = 24 bytes). Mirrors the `MPTokenIssuanceID` field of XRPL `MPTIssuance` and `MPToken` ledger objects (XLS-33d). Useful for multi-issuance scenarios where a single investor holds authorisation for multiple regulated asset pools via separate `MPTokenAuthorize` transactions.

xrplPermissionedDomainId.description: XRPL Permissioned Domain identifier (Hash256) that this wallet is registered or authorised within (XLS-80). Permissioned Domains are controlled environments on the XRP Ledger used to restrict access to Permissioned DEXes, lending protocols, and other compliance-gated services. The domain owner defines which credential types are required for membership. This field links the investor's wallet to the specific on-ledger domain gate, enabling a VASP or asset manager to confirm that the holder satisfies domain-level access controls. Pattern: exactly 64 hex characters (32 bytes, case-insensitive).

xrplAuthorizedCredentialTypes.description: Array of XRPL credential type hex strings that have been accepted and authorised within the Permissioned Domain identified by `xrplPermissionedDomainId` (XLS-80). Each entry follows the same hex-encoding convention as `xrplCredentialType` (1–64 bytes, 2–128 hex characters). A domain may require holders to satisfy multiple credential types simultaneously (e.g. KYC + AML + AccreditedInvestor) before gaining access. This field records which credential types the issuer has confirmed are satisfied for this wallet in this domain.

xrplConfidentialTransfer.description: Configuration record for XLS-96 Confidential Transfers applied to the MPT issuance associated with this wallet. XLS-96 encrypts MPT balances and transfer amounts using EC-ElGamal combined with Zero-Knowledge Proofs, so that validators can enforce supply limits and prevent double-spends without seeing actual amounts. Issuers and designated auditors hold decryption keys for selective disclosure; regulators receive visibility only when legally required. This object carries the public-key material and scheme identifier needed to support selective disclosure to authorised parties.

enabled.description: Whether the MPT issuance associated with this wallet uses XLS-96 confidential transfers. When true, on-ledger balances and transfer amounts are encrypted; the remaining fields in this object carry the selective disclosure key material.

encryptionScheme.description: Encryption scheme used for confidential transfers. 'EC_ELGAMAL' is the XLS-96 scheme (EC-ElGamal + ZKP). Additional schemes may be defined as the standard evolves.

auditorPublicKey.description: EC public key of the designated auditor who holds a decryption key for selective disclosure of encrypted MPT balances and transfer amounts. Encoded as a compressed public key in hexadecimal (66 hex characters = 33 bytes for secp256k1/Ed25519) or base58. Required when enabled is true and an auditor has been designated.

regulatorPublicKey.description: EC public key of the designated regulator who may receive selective disclosure of encrypted balances when legally required. Optional — only populated when a specific regulatory authority has been assigned decryption rights for this issuance. Same encoding as auditorPublicKey.

mptFlags.description: Bitmask of MPTIssuance flags from the XLS-33d Multi-Purpose Token standard, reflecting the compliance configuration of the token issuance. Key flag values: lsfMPTCanLock (0x0002) — individual holder balances can be frozen; lsfMPTRequireAuth (0x0004) — holders must be individually authorised (KYC gate); lsfMPTCanEscrow (0x0008) — tokens can be placed in escrow; lsfMPTCanTrade (0x0010) — tokens are tradeable on DEX; lsfMPTCanTransfer (0x0020) — tokens can transfer between non-issuer accounts; lsfMPTCanClawback (0x0040) — issuer can claw back tokens. Recording the flags enables downstream systems to derive the applicable compliance enforcement model without querying the ledger.

mptMetadata.description: Issuer-defined metadata embedded in the MPTIssuance object (XLS-33d `MPTokenMetadata` field, up to 1024 bytes). Typically carries a URI to the token's prospectus or term sheet, an ISIN, asset class, or a JSON-LD token descriptor that embeds compliance rules directly in the token at the protocol level. May be hex-encoded bytes or a UTF-8 URI string. Mirrors the 'compliance rules in token' pattern described in the Multi-Purpose Token standard for institutional RWA issuance.

mptTransferFee.description: Transfer fee configured on the MPT issuance, in units of 1/1,000,000 (millionths). For example, a value of 1000 represents a 0.1% transfer fee. Mirrors the `TransferFee` field on the `MPTIssuance` ledger object (XLS-33d). Range: 0 (no fee) to 50000 (5%). Recording this field enables fee-aware compliance calculations in off-chain systems.

xrplFreezeType.description: XRPL freeze type applied to this wallet or its token holdings, providing more granular freeze state than the boolean `isFrozen` field. Maps to XRPL's native freeze hierarchy: INDIVIDUAL_FREEZE — only this specific wallet's trustline or MPT holding is frozen (operator or issuer action); GLOBAL_FREEZE — the issuer has frozen all trustlines or MPT holdings for the asset globally; DEEP_FREEZE (XLS-77) — an enhanced freeze that prevents the holder from even receiving tokens or having frozen funds released without issuer action, providing stronger enforcement capability equivalent to ERC-3643 `forcedTransfer()`. When set, `isFrozen` should also be true.

xrplClawbackEnabled.description: Whether the XRPL Clawback amendment is enabled on the issuer account for the trustline or MPT issuance associated with this wallet. When true, the issuer may claw back tokens from this holder using the `Clawback` transaction (native XRPL Clawback amendment) or `MPTokenIssuanceSet` with the clawback flag. This is the XRPL protocol-level equivalent of ERC-3643 `forcedTransfer()`. Recording this flag allows off-chain compliance systems to understand the issuer's enforcement rights without querying ledger state.

lightningNodePubkey.description: Lightning Network node public key (compressed secp256k1 public key, 33 bytes = 66 hex characters) associated with this wallet entry. Used to bind a KYC identity to a Lightning node for LNURL-auth identity verification or Lightning Service Provider (LSP) compliance workflows. Applicable when `network` is 'lightning' or 'bitcoin'. In the OpenKYCAML three-tier enforcement model, Bitcoin and Lightning compliance is enforced at the service layer (Tier 3) rather than at the protocol or smart contract level.

lightningServiceProvider.description: Name or DID of the Lightning Service Provider (LSP) that supplies the compliance wrapper for this Lightning node or Bitcoin wallet. Examples: 'Lightspark' (Lightspark Compliance API), 'Voltage' (Voltage Flow), 'BitGo', 'Coinbase Lightning'. For DID-based LSP references, use a `did:web` or `did:key` URI. This field records which service-layer entity is responsible for AML/KYC enforcement on the Lightning or Bitcoin side, consistent with the OpenKYCAML Tier 3 (service-layer enforcement) model.

bitcoinScriptType.description: Bitcoin script type for the address in the `address` field. Used to provide chain analysis context and determine the applicable UTXO model for compliance screening. P2PKH — Pay to Public Key Hash (legacy); P2WPKH — Pay to Witness Public Key Hash (native SegWit); P2TR — Pay to Taproot (Taproot, BIP-341). Applicable when `network` is 'bitcoin' or a Bitcoin Layer-2 network (Liquid, RGB). Not applicable for Lightning channel addresses (use `lightningNodePubkey` instead).

TrustedIssuer.title: TrustedIssuer
TrustedIssuer.description: An entry in the issuer allowlist used for eIDAS-qualified TSP validation. Relying parties check that the OpenKYCAML credential issuer DID appears in this list and (where trustFramework is eIDAS_2.0) on the applicable EU Trusted List (TSL) before accepting a credential for CDD reliance.

issuerId.description: DID or URI uniquely identifying the issuer. Must match verifiableCredential.issuer.id exactly. Examples: 'did:web:pid-provider.bsi.example.de', 'did:ebsi:znHeZWvhAK2FK2Dk1jXNe2G'.

issuerName.description: Human-readable name of the issuer organisation (e.g. 'Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik — PID Provider').

countryCode.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the Member State or jurisdiction responsible for this issuer.

trustFramework.description: Trust framework under which the issuer is accredited.

eIDASAssuranceLevel.description: eIDAS assurance level at which this issuer is accredited to issue credentials. Required when trustFramework is eIDAS_2.0.

qtspServiceType.description: ETSI TS 119 612 TSP Service Type identifier for the qualified trust service provided by this issuer. Used to validate the issuer against the EU Trusted List (TSL). Common values: 'QCertESig' (qualified certificate for electronic signatures), 'QCertESeal' (qualified certificate for electronic seals), 'PIDSQ' (PID issuer qualified).

tspServiceUri.description: URI of the Trusted List (TSL) XML document where this issuer's service entry can be validated. Relying parties SHOULD fetch this TSL and verify that the issuer's service has ServiceStatus 'http://uri.etsi.org/TrstSvc/TrustedList/Svcstatus/granted'. For EU issuers, this will be a Member State TSL linked from the EU LOTL at https://ec.europa.eu/tools/lotl/eu-lotl.xml.

validFrom.description: ISO 8601 date from which this trust list entry is valid. Credentials issued before this date by the listed issuer are not covered.

validUntil.description: ISO 8601 date until which this trust list entry is valid. Credentials issued after this date by the listed issuer require a renewed entry.

credentialTypes.description: Credential types (vct URIs or W3C VC type strings) that this issuer is trusted to issue. If omitted, the issuer is trusted for all OpenKYCAML credential types.

additionalChecks.description: Relying-party-specific validation rules extending the base trust framework check.

requireKeyBinding.description: When true, the relying party MUST verify the holder key binding JWT (SD-JWT `kb-JWT`) proving the credential was presented by its legitimate holder.

requireStatusCheck.description: When true, the relying party MUST verify the credential status (StatusList2021) before accepting it for CDD reliance.

minimumAssuranceLevel.description: Minimum eIDAS assurance level that the issuer must have achieved for the credential to be accepted by this relying party.

GdprSensitivityMetadata.title: GdprSensitivityMetadata
GdprSensitivityMetadata.description: Machine-readable GDPR/AML sensitivity classification for the OpenKYCAML payload or specific fields. Enables automated enforcement of tipping-off protection (AMLR Art. 73 / FATF Rec. 21), GDPR Art. 9/10 criminal-data safeguards, and disclosure-policy rules in EUDI Wallet presentation flows. When present at payload level the classification applies to the entire document; use restrictedFields to narrow scope to specific JSON Pointer paths.

classification.description: Primary sensitivity class. 'standard' — ordinary personal data (GDPR Art. 6). 'sensitive_personal' — special-category data requiring Art. 9 safeguards (e.g. biometrics, health). 'criminal_offence' — data relating to criminal convictions or offences under GDPR Art. 10. 'sar_restricted' — Suspicious Activity Report or STR material; subject to tipping-off prohibition (AMLR Art. 73 / FATF Rec. 21). 'internal_suspicion' — internal AML suspicion flag not yet escalated to a formal SAR. 'confidential_aml' — AML/CFT investigation data protected from broader disclosure.

restrictedFields.description: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) paths to individual fields or sub-objects within this payload that carry the stated classification. When absent the classification applies to the entire payload. Example: ["/kycProfile/sanctionsScreening", "/ivms101/originator"].

items.description: A valid RFC 6901 JSON Pointer such as '/kycProfile/sanctionsScreening'.

tippingOffProtected.description: When true, any disclosure of this payload (or the referenced restrictedFields) MUST NOT reveal the existence of a SAR, STR, or internal suspicion to the data subject or any unauthorised party. Mandatory for classification 'sar_restricted' or 'internal_suspicion'. Wallets and relying parties that receive a VC with this flag set MUST NOT render or share the affected claims without explicit supervisory authority approval.

legalBasis.description: Primary legal basis for processing this data. 'GDPR-Art6-1c' — legal obligation. 'GDPR-Art6-1e' — public task. 'GDPR-Art9-2g' — substantial public interest. 'GDPR-Art10' — criminal-offence data under official authority. 'AMLR-Art55' — AML data-sharing obligation. 'AMLR-Art73' — tipping-off prohibition compliance. 'national_AML_law' — national transposition of AML directives.

retentionPeriod.description: ISO 8601 duration specifying how long this data must be retained. Overrides any retention hint in kycProfile.auditMetadata.dataRetentionDate. AMLR Art. 55 requires a minimum of 5 years ('P5Y'). SAR-related records may require longer retention per national law.

consentRecord.description: Explicit consent record for processing this sensitive data. Required when legalBasis is GDPR-Art6-1a (consent) or where national law requires documented consent for Art. 9/10 data.

consentGiven.description: Whether the data subject has given explicit consent to process this sensitive data.

consentDate.description: ISO 8601 date-time when consent was obtained.

withdrawalPossible.description: Whether the data subject may withdraw consent. For AML/SAR data this is typically false (legal obligation overrides consent withdrawal).

disclosurePolicy.description: Machine-readable policy governing which parties may receive this data. Wallets and relying-party systems SHOULD enforce these rules automatically before presenting or forwarding a credential.

allowedRecipients.description: Identifiers of permitted recipient categories or specific entities. Examples: 'regulated_fi' (any regulated financial institution), 'fiu_only' (Financial Intelligence Unit only), 'did:web:counterparty.example.com' (a specific VASP).

prohibitedRecipients.description: Categories or entities explicitly prohibited from receiving this data. 'data_subject' MUST be set when tippingOffProtected is true to prevent self-disclosure of SAR existence.

requiresExplicitConsent.description: When true, the recipient must obtain explicit consent from the data subject (or supervisory authority approval for SAR data) before accessing the affected fields.

auditReference.description: Opaque reference to the DPO record, internal audit log entry, or SAR case identifier that governs this classification. MUST NOT contain the SAR narrative or case content (tipping-off risk). Recommended format: an opaque UUID or hash. Example: 'sar-case-ref:sha256:e3b0c44298fc1c149afb...'. Maximum 256 characters.

ExtractedAttribute.title: ExtractedAttribute
ExtractedAttribute.description: A single data element extracted from a verification document, with a flag indicating whether the extracted value matches the corresponding asserted field in the IVMS 101 or kycProfile record.

matchesRecord.description: Whether the extracted value matches the corresponding asserted field in the OpenKYCAML record. True = confirmed match; false = discrepancy requiring resolution.

RegistrationAuthorityDetail.title: RegistrationAuthorityDetail
RegistrationAuthorityDetail.description: Structured reference to a GLEIF Registration Authorities List (RAL) entry. Identifies the official national or regional business registry that is the authoritative source of truth for an entity's legal form and registration data, as required by ISO 17442 and the GLEIF Rulebook.

ralCode.description: GLEIF Registration Authorities List (RAL) code uniquely identifying the registry (e.g. 'RA000585' for Companies House UK, 'RA000602' for Handelsregister Germany). See https://www.gleif.org/en/about-lei/code-lists/gleif-registration-authorities-list.

authorityName.description: Human-readable name of the registration authority (e.g. 'Companies House', 'Handelsregister', 'Secretary of State - Delaware').

jurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the jurisdiction governed by this authority, or ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for sub-national registries (e.g. 'GB', 'DE', 'US-DE').

registryUrl.description: Base URL of the public-facing registry portal (e.g. 'https://find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/').

digitalAccessible.description: Whether the registry is digitally accessible for real-time or near-real-time lookups. When false, physical documents are required as the primary evidence per GLEIF LOU practice.

NaturalPersonDocument.title: NaturalPersonDocument
NaturalPersonDocument.description: A government-issued or qualified-credential document examined to verify attributes of a natural person. Covers physical identity documents (passports, national ID cards) and digital credentials (eIDAS 2.0 PID VCs). Each record links the document type to the specific identity attributes it evidenced, supporting FATF Rec. 10 and AMLR Art. 22 CDD requirements.

documentType.description: Standardised type code for the natural person identity document.

documentTitle.description: Human-readable title or local name of the document (e.g. 'Personalausweis', 'Carte nationale d\'identité', 'Biometric Residence Permit').

documentNumber.description: The document number or serial identifier as printed on the document (e.g. passport number, ID card number).

issuingCountry.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the issuing state.

issuingAuthority.description: Name of the government body or authority that issued the document (e.g. 'Her Majesty\'s Passport Office', 'Bundesdruckerei GmbH', 'DVLA').

issueDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which the document was issued.

expiryDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which the document expires or becomes invalid.

verificationDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which the document was examined and verified by the obliged entity or its verification provider.

verificationMethod.description: Method used to verify the document.

assuranceLevel.description: eIDAS assurance level achieved for this document, where applicable.

documentPresence.description: Whether the document was presented physically or as a digital credential.

documentId.description: Canonical portable identifier for this document in inter-system exchange, using the OpenKYCAML Document ID URN scheme: 'urn:openkycaml:doc:{issuing-country}:{doc-type-code}:{subject-ref}:{discriminator}'. Distinct from documentRef (internal storage path) and credentialId (W3C VC URI). Must remain stable across re-shares of the same document. See docs/reference/document-id-convention.md.

documentRef.description: Internal storage URI or content-addressed reference to the document file or scan held by the obliged entity (e.g. 'urn:doc:abc123', 'https://vault.internal/docs/passport-1.pdf'). MUST be treated as sensitive personal data.

credentialId.description: URI of the underlying W3C Verifiable Credential, SD-JWT VC, or ISO mDL document. Required when documentType is EIDAS_PID_CREDENTIAL. Links to the credential in the VC evidence block.

credentialIssuer.description: DID or URL of the authority that issued the digital credential (e.g. national PID Provider DID). Used when documentType is EIDAS_PID_CREDENTIAL.

extractedAttributes.description: Data elements extracted from this document and cross-referenced against the asserted IVMS 101 or kycProfile fields.

LegalEntityDocument.title: LegalEntityDocument
LegalEntityDocument.description: An official document or digital credential examined to verify attributes of a legal entity. Covers incorporation documents, registry extracts, LEI/vLEI credentials, and governance documents. Each record links the document type to the GLEIF Registration Authority and ISO 20275 ELF code it evidenced, supporting ISO 17442, FATF Rec. 24/25, and AMLR Art. 26 requirements.

documentType.description: Standardised type code for the legal entity verification document.

documentTitle.description: Human-readable title of the document (e.g. 'Certificate of Incorporation on Change of Name', 'Handelsregisterauszug', 'PSC Register Extract').

documentNumber.description: Document-level reference number or certificate number (e.g. a Companies House certificate number).

registrationNumber.description: The entity's registration number as recorded in the issuing registry (e.g. '12345678' for UK Companies House). Distinct from documentNumber.

issuingCountry.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the issuing jurisdiction.

issuingAuthority.description: Human-readable name of the issuing authority or registry (e.g. 'Companies House', 'Bundesanzeiger', 'GLEIF').

gleifRegistrationAuthority.description: Structured GLEIF RAL reference for the issuing registry. Provides machine-readable identification of the authoritative registration authority per ISO 17442.

issueDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which the document was issued or the registry extract was generated.

expiryDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which the document or extract expires. Particularly relevant for registry extracts, which typically have a validity window (e.g. 3–6 months).

verificationDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which the document was verified by the obliged entity, its KYB provider, or the LOU.

verifiedByName.description: Name of the LOU, KYB provider, or verification body that verified this document (e.g. 'Bloomberg Finance L.P.', 'Dun & Bradstreet', 'LuxTrust').

documentPresence.description: Whether the document was examined as a physical original/certified copy or as a digital record/credential.

documentId.description: Canonical portable identifier for this document in inter-system exchange, using the OpenKYCAML Document ID URN scheme: 'urn:openkycaml:doc:{issuing-country}:{doc-type-code}:{subject-ref}:{discriminator}'. Distinct from documentRef (internal storage path) and credentialId (W3C VC URI). Must remain stable across re-shares of the same document. See docs/reference/document-id-convention.md.

documentRef.description: Internal storage URI or content-addressed reference to the document file held by the obliged entity. MUST be treated as sensitive business data.

registryUrl.description: Permalink or deep-link to the live registry record for this entity (e.g. 'https://find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/12345678'). Enables real-time re-verification against the authoritative source.

credentialId.description: URI of the underlying W3C Verifiable Credential. Required when documentType is VLEI_CREDENTIAL or LPID_CREDENTIAL.

credentialIssuer.description: DID or URL of the authority that issued the digital credential (e.g. GLEIF vLEI QVI DID, national LPID Provider DID).

vLEICredentialType.description: When documentType is VLEI_CREDENTIAL, the specific vLEI credential role. QVI = Qualified vLEI Issuer credential; OOR = Official Organisational Role credential (legal representatives); ECR = Engagement Context Role credential (authorised employees).

elfCodeVerified.description: The ISO 20275 ELF code that this document confirmed for the entity's legal form (e.g. 'Z13E' for UK PLC, '8888' for US LLC). Cross-references legalPerson.legalFormCode.

extractedAttributes.description: Data elements extracted from this document and cross-referenced against the asserted legalPerson or kycProfile fields.

VerificationDocumentBundle.title: VerificationDocumentBundle
VerificationDocumentBundle.description: A structured, typed bundle of verification documents assembled to evidence the identity of a natural person and/or a legal entity. Satisfies the documentary evidence requirements of ISO 17442, GLEIF LOUs, FATF Rec. 10/24/25, AMLR Art. 22 and 26, and ISO/IEC 17029:2019. The bundle is evaluated for completeness against the `requiredDocumentTypes` list, which is populated based on the entity type and due diligence level.

naturalPersonDocuments.description: Documents verifying natural person identity. At least one primary photo-ID document (PASSPORT, NATIONAL_ID_CARD, or RESIDENCE_PERMIT) SHOULD be present for CDD. For EDD, a PROOF_OF_ADDRESS and a secondary identifier are additionally recommended.

legalEntityDocuments.description: Documents verifying legal entity identity and legal form. For COMPANY type: CERTIFICATE_OF_INCORPORATION, ARTICLES_OF_ASSOCIATION, and REGISTRY_EXTRACT are recommended. For TRUST: TRUST_DEED and ULTIMATE_BENEFICIAL_OWNER_REGISTER. For PARTNERSHIP: PARTNERSHIP_AGREEMENT and REGISTRY_EXTRACT. For FOUNDATION: FOUNDATION_CHARTER and REGISTRY_EXTRACT.

bundleCompleteness.description: Assessment of whether the bundle satisfies the required document types for this customer profile. COMPLETE = all required documents present and verified; PARTIAL = some required documents outstanding; PENDING = document collection in progress.

bundleValidatedBy.description: Name or DID of the person, team, or automated system that assessed the bundle completeness (e.g. 'Compliance Officer – Jane Smith', 'AutoKYB v2.1', 'did:web:kyb.provider.example').

bundleValidationDate.description: ISO 8601 date on which the bundle completeness was last assessed.

requiredDocumentTypes.description: List of document type codes that are required for this customer profile based on entity type, jurisdiction, and due diligence level. Populated by the obliged entity's compliance system to indicate what the bundle must contain. See VerificationDocumentBundle description for entity-type guidance.

RiskSnapshot.title: RiskSnapshot
RiskSnapshot.description: A point-in-time snapshot of a customer's overall risk score for trend analysis and continuous KYC.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 date-time when this snapshot was captured.

overallRiskScore.description: Composite risk score at this point in time (0 = lowest risk, 100 = highest risk).

deltaFromPrevious.description: Change in overall risk score compared to the immediately prior snapshot. Positive = increased risk, negative = decreased risk.

DataAggregationMetadata.title: DataAggregationMetadata
DataAggregationMetadata.description: BCBS 239 risk data aggregation metadata providing provenance and lineage traceability for regulatory reporting.

bcbs239ComplianceLevel.description: Self-assessed BCBS 239 compliance level for this data aggregation: 'full' (all 14 principles met), 'partial' (some gaps), 'gap-analysis' (under assessment).

lastAggregationTimestamp.description: ISO 8601 date-time of the most recent data aggregation run that produced this payload.

dataLineageReference.description: Opaque reference to the data lineage or provenance record (e.g. a data catalogue entry URI, ETL job ID, or audit log reference). Supports BCBS 239 Principle 2 (Data Architecture and IT Infrastructure) and Principle 3 (Accuracy and Integrity).

PredictiveAML.title: PredictiveAML
PredictiveAML.description: Predictive AML extension providing ML-ready, explainable, auditable risk scores and model metadata. Supports EU AI Act Art. 13 explainability, AMLR Art. 22(5) third-party reliance with provenance, and BCBS 239 data aggregation. Designed for integration with Fenergo, Actimize/NICE, and graph-based network risk engines.

modelMetadata.description: EU AI Act-aligned model card metadata (Art. 13 transparency obligations for high-risk AI systems in AML).

modelId.description: Unique identifier for the AML model (e.g. UUID or vendor model reference).

provider.description: Name or DID of the model provider / vendor.

trainingDate.description: ISO 8601 date-time when the model was last trained.

euAiActClassification.description: EU AI Act Annex III classification. AML transaction monitoring and customer risk scoring systems are presumed 'high-risk-aml' under Art. 6(2) and Annex III point 5(b).

conformityAssessmentReference.description: Reference to the EU AI Act conformity assessment record (Art. 43) or notified body certificate number. Required when euAiActClassification = 'high-risk-aml'.

predictiveScores.description: Array of typed predictive risk scores produced by the model.

value.description: Score value on a 0–100 scale (0 = lowest risk, 100 = highest risk).

confidence.description: Model confidence in this score (0–1). EU AI Act Art. 13(3)(b)(iv) requires confidence or reliability information.

horizonDays.description: Forward-looking horizon in calendar days over which the score is predictive (e.g. 30 = 30-day anomaly probability).

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 date-time when this score was computed.

featureVector.description: Optional hashed or privacy-preserved feature vector used to generate the scores (e.g. velocity_30d, avg_txn_value). Keys are feature names; values are numeric, string, or boolean. Personal data MUST be hashed or pseudonymised before inclusion (GDPR Art. 25 data minimisation).

explainability.description: EU AI Act Art. 13 explainability output. Provides human-interpretable reasoning for the predictive scores.

method.description: Explainability method used. SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) are recommended for AML models.

values.description: Method-specific explanation output (e.g. SHAP feature importance values keyed by feature name). Structure is method-dependent.

riskEvolutionHistory.description: Ordered history of overall risk snapshots enabling delta-based continuous KYC (cKYC). Supports AMLR Art. 22 ongoing monitoring and avoids unnecessary full re-KYC when risk has not materially changed.

alertCorrelationId.description: Opaque correlation identifier linking this predictive payload to a transaction monitoring alert (e.g. Actimize case ID). Enables closed-loop feedback from alert disposition to model retrain.

InformationSharingFlags.title: InformationSharingFlags
InformationSharingFlags.description: USA PATRIOT Act Section 314(a) (31 CFR 1010.520) and Section 314(b) (31 CFR 1010.540) information sharing flags. Section 314(a) supports mandatory law-enforcement information requests; Section 314(b) enables voluntary institution-to-institution sharing with safe-harbor protection.

section314aRequestId.description: FinCEN Section 314(a) request identifier. Presence indicates this customer record is subject to a mandatory FinCEN information request. MUST NOT be disclosed to the data subject (tipping-off prohibition).

section314bSafeHarbor.description: When true, this record was shared under the FinCEN Section 314(b) voluntary information sharing safe-harbor. The sharing institution must be a Section 314(b) registrant and sharing must be for AML purposes (31 CFR 1010.540).

sharingConsentTimestamp.description: ISO 8601 date-time when consent for information sharing was obtained or the safe-harbor sharing event occurred.

LegacyIdentifiers.title: LegacyIdentifiers
LegacyIdentifiers.description: Optional block for legacy PKI and directory identifiers. Carries X.500 Distinguished Names (DNs) used as Subject/Issuer fields in X.509 certificates and as directory / SWIFTNet addresses. X.400 O/R Addresses are explicitly excluded from this schema (see docs/mappings/x500-x400-legacy.md).

x500DN.description: X.500 Distinguished Name in RFC 4514 string representation (e.g. CN=John Doe,OU=Finance,O=AcmeBank,C=GB). Used as the Subject or Issuer of an X.509 certificate, as a SWIFTNet technical address (cn=...,ou=...,o=...,o=swift), or as an LDAP directory entry reference. eIDAS 2.0 QES/QSealC/QWAC/QEAA certificates MUST carry an X.500 DN in the certificate Subject field (ETSI EN 319 412 series).

x500DNType.description: Context in which the X.500 DN is used. 'certificateSubject' — DN appears in the Subject field of an X.509 certificate. 'certificateIssuer' — DN appears in the Issuer field of an X.509 certificate. 'directoryEntry' — DN identifies an entry in an LDAP/X.500 directory. 'swiftNetAddress' — DN is a SWIFTNet technical addressing DN (cn=..,o=swift format).

PkiEvidence.title: PkiEvidence
PkiEvidence.description: Optional PKI evidence block. Captures metadata from an X.509 public-key or attribute certificate (ITU-T X.509 / ISO/IEC 9594-8) used to evidence the identity of a natural person or legal entity. Supports eIDAS 2.0 Qualified Electronic Certificates (QES, QSealC, QWAC, QEAA), ETSI EN 319 412 QTSP certificates, and SWIFTNet Identifiers Directory entries. Links to legacyIdentifiers.x500DN for the Subject / Issuer DN and to identityDocuments for the full document bundle.

x509Certificate.title: X509Certificate
x509Certificate.description: Metadata extracted from an X.509 v3 certificate. All fields are optional; include as many as are available from the certificate. The serialNumber + issuerDN pair uniquely identifies the certificate per RFC 5280.

serialNumber.description: Certificate serial number as a hex string (RFC 5280 §4.1.2.2). Must be unique within the issuer's namespace.

subjectDN.description: X.500 Distinguished Name of the certificate subject in RFC 4514 string format. SHOULD match legacyIdentifiers.x500DN when x500DNType is 'certificateSubject'.

issuerDN.description: X.500 Distinguished Name of the certificate issuer (CA / QTSP) in RFC 4514 string format.

validFrom.description: Certificate validity start (notBefore field, RFC 5280 §4.1.2.5) in ISO 8601 format.

validTo.description: Certificate validity end (notAfter field, RFC 5280 §4.1.2.5) in ISO 8601 format. Relying parties MUST reject certificates past this date unless a valid OCSP staple or CRL entry confirms continuation.

signatureAlgorithm.description: Signature algorithm OID or well-known name (e.g. 'sha256WithRSAEncryption', 'ecdsa-with-SHA256', 'id-RSASSA-PSS'). Corresponds to the certificate signatureAlgorithm field (RFC 5280 §4.1.1.2).

qcStatements.description: List of ETSI EN 319 412 / RFC 3739 QCStatement OIDs or well-known short names present in the certificate's id-pe-QCStatements extension (OID 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.1.3). Examples: 'id-etsi-qcs-QcCompliance' (QES), 'id-etsi-qcs-QcSSCD' (QSCD / secure device), 'id-etsi-qcs-QcType-eSign', 'id-etsi-qcs-QcType-eSeal', 'id-etsi-qcs-QcType-Web' (QWAC), 'id-etsi-qcs-QcPDS' (policy disclosure statement URL), 'id-etsi-qcs-QcCClegislation' (jurisdiction of QC).

crlDistributionPoints.description: URIs of CRL Distribution Points (RFC 5280 §4.2.1.13). Relying parties use these to download the issuer's Certificate Revocation List and confirm the certificate has not been revoked.

ocspResponderUrl.description: URL of the OCSP (Online Certificate Status Protocol, RFC 6960) responder for real-time revocation status checking. Preferred over CRL for eIDAS Qualified Certificates (ETSI EN 319 411).

thumbprintSha256.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the DER-encoded certificate as a lowercase hex string (64 characters). Provides a stable unique identifier for the certificate that is independent of its serial number.

oids.description: ASN.1 Object Identifiers (OIDs) extracted from X.509 extensions, ETSI profiles, or other PKI structures. Use to surface policy OIDs, ETSI EN 319 412 QCStatement details, or custom extension values for machine-readable automated validation by relying parties.

oid.description: Dotted-decimal OID string (e.g. '1.3.6.1.5.5.7.1.3' for id-pe-QCStatements). Must match the ASN.1 object-identifier notation defined in ITU-T X.660.

description.description: Human-readable name or short description of the OID (e.g. 'id-etsi-qcs-QcCompliance', 'id-pe-subjectInfoAccessSyntax').

value.description: Optional decoded value of the OID extension. May be a string (for simple text values) or a structured object (for complex ASN.1 sequences). For QCStatements, the value may include policy disclosure statement (PDS) URIs or jurisdiction codes.

certificateDocumentRef.description: Reference to the corresponding document record in identityDocuments.legalEntityDocuments[] or identityDocuments.naturalPersonDocuments[] by documentRef URI. Enables full eIDAS triangulation: certificate metadata here links to the document bundle that holds the source credential and its extracted identity attributes.

$schema.description: Reference to the OpenKYCAML JSON Schema URI.

messageId.description: Unique message identifier for deduplication and tracing.

messageDateTime.description: ISO 8601 datetime when this message was created.

ivms101.description: IVMS 101 Travel Rule payload. Required when transmitting under FATF Recommendation 16.

verifiableCredential.description: Optional W3C Verifiable Credential wrapper for eIDAS 2.0 / EUDIW-compatible attestation.

kycProfile.description: Optional KYC/AML profile providing full customer risk data for CDD reliance, EDD, and cross-system interoperability under AMLR 2027.

transactionMonitoring.description: Optional transaction-level AML monitoring results, rule evaluations, travel-rule compliance status, and reporting obligations for this specific transaction.

trustedIssuers.description: Optional allowlist of trusted credential issuers with eIDAS qualified TSP validation rules. Relying parties use this list to validate that the verifiableCredential.issuer appears on the applicable EU Trusted List (TSL) before accepting a credential for CDD reliance under AMLR Art. 22 and eIDAS 2.0.

gdprSensitivityMetadata.description: Optional GDPR/AML sensitivity classification block. Provides machine-readable tipping-off protection flags (AMLR Art. 73 / FATF Rec. 21), legal-basis references (GDPR Art. 9/10), retention rules, and disclosure policies. When present, wallets and relying parties MUST apply the stated classification before rendering or forwarding any credential fields.

identityDocuments.description: Optional verification document bundle. Contains the typed, named set of government-issued documents and digital credentials examined to verify natural person and/or legal entity identity. Satisfies ISO 17442 (LEI), GLEIF LOU documentary evidence requirements, FATF Rec. 10/24/25, and AMLR Art. 22 and 26. All changes are additive; omitting this block does not affect v1.4.0 payload validity.

predictiveAML.description: Optional predictive AML extension providing ML-ready, explainable, auditable risk scores. See PredictiveAML $def for full schema.

informationSharingFlags.description: USA PATRIOT Act Section 314(a)/(b) (31 CFR 1010.520/540) information sharing flags. Optional; include only when a 314(a) request or 314(b) safe-harbor sharing event applies.

legacyIdentifiers.description: Optional legacy PKI identifier block. Carries X.500 Distinguished Names used in X.509 certificate Subject/Issuer fields, SWIFTNet technical addresses, and LDAP/X.500 directory entries. Enables direct linkage of identity evidence to eIDAS-compliant Qualified Certificates (AMLR Art. 22(6) & eIDAS Art. 24). X.400 O/R Addresses are explicitly excluded — see docs/mappings/x500-x400-legacy.md.

pkiEvidence.description: Optional X.509 PKI evidence block. Captures certificate metadata (serial number, Subject/Issuer DNs, validity window, QCStatements, CRL/OCSP endpoints, SHA-256 thumbprint) and ASN.1 OIDs from ETSI EN 319 412 profiles. Satisfies AMLR Art. 22 & 56 PKI evidence requirements and enables predictive AML cert-provenance tracking (issuance/revocation timestamps in riskEvolutionHistory).
